programmes.show_description: Incredible Survivors: From plane crashes to superhuman feats, William Shatner checks out those who lived through the impossible. (S1, ep 8)
Hawaii Volcanoes National Park: The Hawaii Volcanoes National Park is the site of strange events. Are they linked to the legendary Native Hawaiian Goddess of the Volcano, Pele? (S1, ep 3)
Joshua Tree National Park: Are UFO sightings at Joshua Tree linked to an anti-aging machine and a powerful creature called the Yucca Man? (S1, ep 4)
Could history's most powerful men know more about extra-terrestrials than us? Experts explore the complex relationship between presidents and aliens. (S1, ep 13)
Experts scrutinise the engineering behind the Egyptians' Sphinx and pyramids. Could this advanced civilisation have received extra-terrestrial help? (S1, ep 14)
Alien Origins: The compelling series returns to examine more eerie reports of UFOs. Can it determine where in the universe such strange flying crafts could be coming from? (S3, ep 1)
Astronaut Encounters: To this day, NASA denies all UFO accounts and has taken measures to prevent its astronauts from telling their stories. But what are they hiding? (S3, ep 3)
Baby on Board!: Two men attempt a carjacking at a Louisiana car wash. A hapless teenage driver turns an Alabama parking lot into a demolition derby. (S1, ep 9)
Seth's Secret: Les feels hurt when he finds out that Seth is at a rival pawn shop and fears that he is quitting American Jewelry to join the competition. (S10, ep 13)
Breakdown at Tiffany's: A man brings in a 160-year-old Tiffany watch. But with no official markings on it, will Les be tempted by his $40,000 asking price? (S10, ep 9)
Harold's Gamble: Les offers to buy a diamond-encrusted Audemars Piguet watch from a customer but asks his watch expert take a closer look at it first. (S10, ep 11)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Oakley Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 9)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Sam Stratford Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 10)
Episode 8: Henry and Skid visit moped fan Dave, scoop up fresh treasures, brave bad weather and chase more finds at FM Hutchings before selling for profit. (S4, ep 8)
Fiat 126: The classic Fiat 500 is too expensive, so the team turn their attention to a rust-free Fiat 126 - but the complications turn into a huge nightmare. (S3, ep 9)
Pawntourage: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Chum does battle over a jacket and a Johnny Drama whiskey bottle tries to join Chum's entourage. (S24, ep 9)
Fender Vendor: A woman brings in a Japanese matchlock rifle that weighs almost as much as she does, followed by two classics: a Superman figure and a Tadeo Fender Telecaster. (S24, ep 8)
Pucks For Bucks: The guys get stuck with a unit they didn't want and have to sort through trash to make their bottom line. (S5, ep 16)
Whiskey Business: An auction in Allen's old stomping grounds triggers memories of being a 'lone wolf.' The guys find a set of RC race boats. (S5, ep 17)
Haff-Inated: Allen's off his game without his morning coffee, but some fun items help him re-energise. The guys find a wrestling ring and vintage animatronics. (S5, ep 18)
Brew-Phoria: Ton and Allen go all in for an electric car, but with the batteries missing, a pricey refurb is their only shot at a profit. (S5, ep 19)
Catch My Drift?: The guys land a custom drift car and a luxury antiques unit that could have a one-of-a-kind pilot pistol from WWI. (S5, ep 20)
The Wild West: Allen Haff and Clinton 'Ton' Jones head to San Bernadino in search of a storage locker gold rush. Ton finds a 1880s gun, but it's only valuable if it fires. (S1, ep 1)
The Gutfather: Rene gets superstitious as he hopes to strike it lucky for a second time at auctions in Fontana. Ivy goes with his gut. But will his instincts pay off? (S6, ep 17)
Grin And Barry It: Barry sets out to humiliate Ivy in the next set of auctions. Rene is in for a nasty surprise, while Jarrod and Brandi find themselves surrounded by pigs. (S6, ep 14)
The Adventures of Max Profit: Ivy is on the hunt for high-end items in Moreno Valley, while Jarrod reveals to Brandi an alter ego known as Max Profit. (S6, ep 15)
Follow all the drama as the buyers bid on more abandoned storage lockers. What treasures lie inside and can the dealers sell them on for a profit? (S6, ep 16)
Pay The Dan: Ivy gets excited by the prospect of high-end lockers. Rene and Casey struggle to agree and Darrell tries to distract his rivals with some old-fashioned pranks. (S6, ep 17)
Robot Cowboy: The pressure falls on Darrell and Brandon to drum up merchandise for their website. Plus, Jarrod and Brandi put a bidding strategy to the test. Will it work? (S6, ep 18)
Swamp Things: The team tests Chris Bledsoe, from Florida's Everglades to Virginia's Monroe Institute, seeking how his brain uniquely communicates. (S3, ep 3)
Smoke it out: The team ignites a massive bonfire to investigate the Bubble. But what appears in the smoke is more chilling than anyone expected. (S6, ep 3)
Beyond the Breakers: Jeff and the team embark on a journey in pursuit of a cache of precious gemstones. But with bad weather closing in, can they find the wreck of the Neptune? (S1, ep 6)
Empress of Britain: A fabulous cruise ship for the super wealthy, the Empress of Britain was called up for war duty and entered an epic final battle with the German Luftwaffe. (S2, ep 5)
An Oregon woman doing some work on a remote cabin is spied on by a dark figure. Next, a man in Arkansas encounters something terrifying in the woods. (S8, ep 15)
In Florida, an investigator captures dark shadow figures while walking through a cemetery and a young girl in Ohio sees the ghost of her late grandfather. (S8, ep 16)
Mutant Mermaid, UFOs vs Nukes and Seamonster Chases a Boat: Is a dead mermaid really preserved in Japan? And are UFOs attracted to nuclear facilities? (S3, ep 19)
Alien Sabotage, Ghost Child and Hungry Hungry Sasquatch: Strange creatures are encountered and a home security video may be proof that UFOs are messing with technology. (S3, ep 20)
The UFO Files: In this special, Tony Harris reviews the latest developments and most compelling photographic evidence from a tumultuous year of UFO revelations. (S4, ep 8)
Medieval Lincoln: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world. (S3, ep 1)
Restoration London: Alice heads to London to attempt to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. (S3, ep 2)
Bradford 1: David Dickinson's team are in Bradford. Tim is brimming over a Lalique bowl. Will David Hakeney let a vintage train set go to auction for the sake of a tenner? (S10, ep 20)
Roadmaster Rick: Rick and Corey discover an old Buick Roadmaster, Chum checks out a doll and volleyball signed by Tom Hanks and a samurai sword lands on the counter. (S24, ep 10)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective.The Stratford Wreck- Trospective: Henry's Wreck-trospective dives headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the series. (S1, ep 2)
The Mom Factor: Jarrod and Brandi have some fun as they attempt to beat the competition. Meanwhile, Casey tries her hand at bidding. (S6, ep 20)
The Emperor of El Monte: Buyers Darrell, Brandon, Jarrod, Brandi and Ivy head to El Monte, California where they tackle the local competition head on. (S7, ep 9)
Judgment Day: The buyers are in for the fight of their lives as they lock horns at the nation's biggest shipping container auction. (S1, ep 21)
Bid or Die: Newbies Scott and Chrissy push their way onto Brandon and Lori's turf, forcing the couple to burn cash like never before. (S2, ep 1)
Sailing Away: The Israelis go after what they think is vintage clothing. Meanwhile, a custom container could land a buyer in jail. (S2, ep 5)
The Winter Games: Containers bound for the games in Russia miss the boat. A waterlogged container piques everyone's interest. (S2, ep 6)
Wild Drums: Jake lets his pack loose on a mountain lion and comes face-to-face with a bear. Tom and Jack build traditional drums as they prepare to reopen the trading post. (S11, ep 11)
Bubble Trouble: As the team relaunches their investigation on Skinwalker Ranch, they are surrounded by a shocking phenomenon. (S6, ep 1)
Pawntourage: Things go nuclear when a Japanese Godzilla toy hits the counter. Chum does battle over a jacket and a Johnny Drama whiskey bottle tries to join Chum's entourage. (S24, ep 9)
Deep In The Heart Of Upland: A surprise visitor from Texas shows up in time for a series of heated auctions in Upland, where a bad day for Ivy ends with a big bang. (S7, ep 5)
Daneurysm: Brandi struggles to keep Jarrod focused at abandoned locker auctions in Hemet. Meanwhile, Dan and Laura Dotson find themselves faced with a life or death crisis. (S7, ep 6)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Bromley Wreck-Trospective: Get ready for a high-octane nostalgia trip as Junk & Disorderly Henry's Wreck-trospective dives into the archives.(S1, ep 3)
The Top Ten Mysteries of the Deep: Count down the top ten mysteries of the deep, from sunken continents to supposed proof of the lost city of Atlantis. (S15, ep 17)
Searching For Bigfoot: Experts investigate why sightings of Bigfoot are so often accompanied by reports of UFOs. Could the Sasquatch be an alien living in our midst? (S1, ep 5)
Judgment Day: The buyers are in for the fight of their lives as they lock horns at the nation's biggest shipping container auction. (S1, ep 21)
Bid or Die: Newbies Scott and Chrissy push their way onto Brandon and Lori's turf, forcing the couple to burn cash like never before. (S2, ep 1)
The Mom Factor: Jarrod and Brandi have some fun as they attempt to beat the competition. Meanwhile, Casey tries her hand at bidding. (S6, ep 20)
The Emperor of El Monte: Buyers Darrell, Brandon, Jarrod, Brandi and Ivy head to El Monte, California where they tackle the local competition head on. (S7, ep 9)
Bradford 2: David Dickinson's team are in Bradford. Tim is brimming over a Lalique bowl. Will David Hakeney let a vintage train set go to auction for the sake of a tenner? (S10, ep 21)
Bursting the Bubble: As the team launches high-powered rockets into the sky on Skinwalker Ranch, they are frozen in shock by what they encounter. (S6, ep 4)
Chip off the Orb Block: Notorious phenomenon-experiencer Chris Bledsoe's daughter Emily may share his abilities and they face the Monroe Institute and the Everglades. (S3, ep 4)
Health and Wealth: With the help of modern science, uncover the secrets of health and wealth in Ancient Egypt from Queen Hatshepsut's death to a case of breast cancer. (S1, ep 5)
